bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with their worst possible offer (all valueE = 0.2 utility each, giving me a total utility of 0.2). Their urgency message is fake pressure at t=0.00 — we're at the very start of negotiation. I should open with my best offer (all valueA) to anchor high, as this is the opening move and there's no reason to concede yet.

The opponent is repeating their worst-case offer (all valueE) and using fake urgency tactics. At t=0.02 we're very early in the negotiation, so there's no reason to concede. Their messages are classic pressure tactics ("closing window," "deadlock") which I should ignore. I'll maintain a strong bid close to my maximum utility, perhaps making a tiny concession on the lowest-weight issues (issueD and issueE) to signal willingness to negotiate while keeping high values on issueA and issueB (both weight 0.3).

We're only at t=0.04, very early in the negotiation. The opponent is using fake urgency tactics ("window is closing") but their offers are extremely low for me (all valueE = utility 0.20 each = total 0.20). They only moved issueA from valueE to valueD in their latest bid, which is minimal concession. I should stay near my optimal but make slight concessions on low-weight issues (issueD, issueE) to show good faith. I'll keep valueA on issueA and issueB (weight 0.3 each), and concede slightly on issueC, issueD, issueE.

We're very early (t=0.06) so I can still bid close to my preferred values. The opponent is stuck at mostly valueE across all issues, barely conceding (only moved issueA from valueE to valueD after 3 rounds). Previous bids: t=0.04 I offered valueC on issueE and valueB on issueD, so I can slightly vary while still maintaining high utility.
